documentation:
plugin utils

plugin configuration structure:

[section_name]
plugin_sequence = <plugin_name1>,<plugin_name2>
plugin.<plugin_name> = <plugin.lib.path.ClassName>
plugin.<plugin_name>.<attr1> = <value1>
plugin.<plugin_name>.<attr2> = <value2>

modules.name: lib.idds.common.exceptions
documentation:
IDDS Exceptions.

error codes:
    The fist the number is one of the main catagories.
    The second number is one of the subcatagories.
    The third number and numbers after third one are local defined for every subcatagory.

Catagories:
 1. common/unknown IDDS exception
 2. ORM related exception
    1. request table related exception
    2. transform table related exception
    3. collection table related exception
    4. collection_content table related exception
    5. processing table related exception
    6. messages table related exception
 3. core related exception
 4. Rest related exception
    1. bad request
    2. connection error
 5. Agent exception
    1. No plugin exception
    2. Agent plugin exception
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

classes.name: lib.idds.common.exceptions.IDDSException
documentation:
To correctly use this class, inherit from it and define
a 'message' property. That message will get printf'd
with the keyword arguments provided to the constructor.

modules.name: lib.idds.common.config
documentation:
Configurations.

configuration file looking for path:
    1. $IDDS_CONFIG
    2. $IDDS_HOME/etc/idds/idds.cfg
    3. /etc/idds/idds.cfg
    4. $VIRTUAL_ENV/etc/idds/idds.cfg

documentation: Return function name from a function.

functions.name: lib.idds.common.imports.import_func
documentation:
Returns a function from a dotted path name. Example: `path.to.module:func`.

When the attribute we look for is a staticmethod, module name in its
dotted path is not the last-before-end word

E.g.: filename:module_a.module_b:ClassA.my_static_method

Thus we remove the bits from the end of the name until we can import it

Args:
    name (str): The name (reference) to the path.

Raises:
    ValueError: If no module is found or invalid attribute name.

Returns:
    Any: An attribute (normally a Callable)

functions.name: lib.idds.common.imports.import_attribute
documentation:
Returns an attribute from a dotted path name. Example: `path.to.func`.

When the attribute we look for is a staticmethod, module name in its
dotted path is not the last-before-end word

E.g.: package_a.package_b.module_a.ClassA.my_static_method

Thus we remove the bits from the end of the name until we can import it

Args:
    name (str): The name (reference) to the path.

Raises:
    ValueError: If no module is found or invalid attribute name.

Returns:
    Any: An attribute (normally a Callable)

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.kill_process_group
documentation:
Kill the process group.
DO NOT MOVE TO PROCESSES.PY - will lead to circular import since execute() needs it as well.
\:param pgrp: process group id (int).
\:param nap: napping time between kill signals in seconds (int)
\:return: boolean (True if SIGTERM followed by SIGKILL signalling was successful)

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.kill_all
documentation:
Kill all processes after a time-out exception in process.communication().

```
param process

process object

return

stderr (str).
```

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.get_parameters_from_string
documentation:
Find all strings starting with '%'. For example, for this string below, it should return ['NUM_POINTS', 'IN', 'OUT']
'run --rm -it -v "$(pwd)":/payload gitlab-registry.cern.ch/zhangruihpc/endpointcontainer:latest /bin/bash -c "echo "--num_points %NUM_POINTS"; /bin/cat /payload/%IN>/payload/%OUT"'

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.replace_parameters_with_values
documentation:
Replace all strings starting with '%'. For example, for this string below, it should replace ['%NUM_POINTS', '%IN', '%OUT']
'run --rm -it -v "\$(pwd)":/payload gitlab-registry.cern.ch/zhangruihpc/endpointcontainer:latest /bin/bash -c "echo "--num_points %NUM_POINTS"; /bin/cat /payload/%IN>/payload/%OUT"'

\:param text
\:param values: parameter values, for example {'NUM_POINTS': 5, 'IN': 'input.json', 'OUT': 'output.json'}

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.import_func
documentation:
Returns a function from a dotted path name. Example: `path.to.module:func`.

When the attribute we look for is a staticmethod, module name in its
dotted path is not the last-before-end word

E.g.: package_a.package_b.module_a:ClassA.my_static_method

Thus we remove the bits from the end of the name until we can import it

Args:
    name (str): The name (reference) to the path.

Raises:
    ValueError: If no module is found or invalid attribute name.

Returns:
    Any: An attribute (normally a Callable)

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.import_attribute
documentation:
Returns an attribute from a dotted path name. Example: `path.to.func`.

When the attribute we look for is a staticmethod, module name in its
dotted path is not the last-before-end word

E.g.: package_a.package_b.module_a.ClassA.my_static_method

Thus we remove the bits from the end of the name until we can import it

Args:
    name (str): The name (reference) to the path.

Raises:
    ValueError: If no module is found or invalid attribute name.

Returns:
    Any: An attribute (normally a Callable)

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.modified_environ
documentation:
Temporarily updates the `os.environ` dictionary in-place.
The `os.environ` dictionary is updated in-place so that the modification
is sure to work in all situations.
\:param remove: Environment variables to remove.
\:param update: Dictionary of environment variables and values to add/update.

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.run_with_timeout
documentation:
Run a function with a timeout.

Parameters:
    func (callable): The function to run.
    args (tuple): The arguments to pass to the function.
    kwargs (dict): The keyword arguments to pass to the function.
    timeout (float or int): The time limit in seconds.

Returns:
    The function's result if it finishes within the timeout.
    Raises TimeoutError if the function takes longer than the specified timeout.

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.timeout_wrapper
documentation:
Decorator to timeout a function after a given number of seconds.

Parameters:
    seconds (int or float): The time limit in seconds.

Raises:
    TimeoutError: If the function execution exceeds the time limit.

functions.name: lib.idds.common.utils.run_command_with_timeout
documentation: Run a command and monitor its output. Terminate if no output within timeout.
